# Clang trunk (x86): assertion in TwoAddressInstructionImpl::processTiedPairs (duplicate tied reg) during backend lowering of inline asm with multiple tied outputs/inputs

**Author:** Theaamadu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173615
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** llvm:codegen, crash, inline-asm, generated by fuzzer

## Body

## Compiler Explorer: https://godbolt.org/z/nK9aseMGa

- clang start crashing on `Trunk`
- generated by fuzzer

## Console
```console
clang: /root/llvm-project/llvm/lib/CodeGen/TwoAddressInstructionPass.cpp:1605: void {anonymous}::TwoAddressInstructionImpl::processTiedPairs(llvm::MachineInstr*, {anonymous}::TwoAddressInstructionImpl::TiedPairList&, unsigned int&): Assertion `i == DstIdx || !MI->getOperand(i).isReg() || MI->getOperand(i).getReg() != RegA' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.

```

## Code
```c
void foo(void) {
  unsigned char e[16];
  unsigned long a, b, c, d;
  __asm__ __volatile__(""
                       : "=d"(a), "=&c"(c), "=&D"(d), "=&a"(b)
                       : "D0"(-1U), "mr"(e), "1"(128 >> 5), "2"(e), "3"(-1U));
}
```

## Comments

### Comment 1 - keinflue

Further reduction:
```cpp
void foo(void) {
    int a, b, c;
    asm("" : "=d"(a), "=c"(b), "=D"(c) : "D0"(0), "2"(0));
}
```
Goes back to at least Clang 2.7: https://godbolt.org/z/59fj19Tx5

Similar to issue https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/118247, however I do not see the same structure in the constraints between the test cases after reduction.

---
